summary: Investigated login, wallet, SePay top-up and auction deposit for the new Le-Phuoc-Sang frontend + backend. Root issue of the user's confusion: they were viewing localhost:8096 (backend static test page) instead of the Next.js UI on localhost:3000, and the frontend dev server had stopped. Verified end-to-end via API that all four modules already work: login (JWT), GET /api/wallet, SePay QR (POST /api/wallet/deposit) + webhook crediting (POST /api/wallet/sepay-webhook), deposit eligibility (GET /api/auctions/{id}/eligibility) and placement (POST /api/deposits?auctionId=). Deposit had been failing only because all seeded auctions' 30-min deposit windows had expired (seeded in the morning). Fixes applied: restarted frontend on 3000; SQL-updated all non-ended auctions to future start times so deposit windows are open; marked demo bidders (user/alice/bob @example.com) KYC-verified so deposits aren't blocked; improved DataSeeder seedAuction calls to start auctions 1-8 days out for future fresh seeds. Confirmed full collector flow: walletuser deposit on auction 2 succeeded (balance 5.5M->4.75M, hold 750k LOCKED). All frontend pages (/auth,/wallet,/auctions/1,/auction-room/1) return 200.
current_status: All requested modules work. Backend http://localhost:8096 (DB SWP_Nhom3_App), frontend http://localhost:3000 - open 3000 for the real UI. Demo login password 'password'. Note: restarting backend re-runs DataSeeder which would reset the KYC flags set via SQL for alice/bob/walletuser; auction time fix is data-only (code change covers future fresh seeds). SePay credit is async via webhook.
